name: Mo Alie-Cox
headline: Blocking-first Colt buried behind rookie Tyler Warren
body:
Alie-Cox remains a low-volume, blocking-first tight end, and the 2025 line proves it: 11 games, a career-low 39.1 percent snap share, 173 routes and just 20 targets for 13-117-1 and 17.7 fantasy points, ranked TE63. His 3.8 percent target share and 0.071 WOPR are irrelevant, and a -0.043 EPA per play fell sharply from 2024's 0.358. The lone bright spot is red-zone usage, six red-zone and two end-zone targets, the touchdown-dependent profile that yielded 3 scores and 36.1 points in 2023.

The 2026 outlook gets worse with rookie Tyler Warren installed as Indianapolis's clear TE1. Alie-Cox slides to the TE2 in-line role, where his value is purely as a blocker who occasionally vultures a goal-line target. With route participation already at 30.1 percent and trending down, there is no realistic receiving volume to mine. He is off the fantasy radar in all redraft formats, a deep-bench afterthought only in a rare touchdown-or-bust week.
